metadata.title: Connecting Action Semantics and Human Motion using Fuzzy Qualitative Kinematics
affiliations: School of Computer Science and Technology, Beijing Institute of Technology, China

abstract: The paper proposes fuzzy qualitative kinematics (FQK) and fuzzy qualitative tokens (FQTs) to bridge raw human motion and action semantics, and evaluates a unified motion understanding system across interpolation, prediction, generation, and prompt-related tasks on HumanML3D and KIT-ML.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.summary: Motivates the semantic gap between raw motion and action semantics and introduces fuzzy qualitative tokens as an intermediate representation.

sections.title: Related Work
sections.summary: Covers prior motion representations and motion generation methods, with emphasis on intermediate representations.

sections.title: Method
sections.summary: Defines fuzzy vocabularies, membership functions, 11 FQT families, the token extractor, and a unified motion model.

sections.title: Implementation Details
sections.summary: Reports training schedule, optimizer, latent size, hardware, and task-specific inference setup.

sections.title: Kinematic Prompt Generation
sections.summary: Converts FQTs into text prompts intended to improve interpretability and fine-grained motion control.

sections.title: Experiments
sections.summary: Benchmarks the method on HumanML3D and KIT-ML using quantitative, qualitative, perceptual, and ablation analyses.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.summary: Claims that FQTs improve motion quality, diversity, multimodality, and semantic expressiveness.

sections.title: Limitations and Future Work
sections.summary: Acknowledges vocabulary-size trade-offs, possible new attributes/tokens, and translation ambiguity.

parse_alerts.message: Token labels used in Table I and the ablation section do not align with the formal token names defined in the method.
The paper states 11 FQTs in the method but says the extractor handles ten types elsewhere.
The manuscript contains an unresolved citation placeholder '[REF]'.
References include visible duplicates and inconsistent normalization.
Training/evaluation preprocessing rates conflict across sections (30Hz vs 15Hz).

normalization_notes: This enriched artifact is evidence-grounded from the provided PDF and retains uncertainty where the source paper is internally inconsistent.
Claims are summarized conservatively; unresolved notation and reference issues are preserved as parse alerts rather than silently corrected.
Reference extraction is intentionally partial because bibliography integrity problems are themselves part of the review evidence.
